entries.source: "Ask me one question that helps me start."
entries.value: "Tambaya me one tambaya that helps me fara."

entries.source: "Do not give the final answer yet. Ask me what I think the first step is."
entries.value: "Kada a give the final amsa yet. tambaya me abin da I think the farko mataki shi ne."

entries.source: "Explain why this step works."
entries.value: "Bayyana me ya sa this mataki works."

entries.source: "Give me a hint, not the final answer."
entries.value: "Give me a alama, not the final amsa."

entries.source: "Give me a similar problem after I solve this one."
entries.value: "Give me a similar problem bayan I warware this one."

entries.source: "Here is my answer. What misconception does it show?"
entries.value: "Here shi ne my amsa. abin da rashin fahimta does it show?"

entries.source: "Here is my attempt. What is the smallest hint that would help?"
entries.value: "Here shi ne my attempt. abin da shi ne the smallest alama that would taimako?"

entries.source: "Make a similar practice problem after this one."
entries.value: "Sa a similar atisaye problem bayan this one."

entries.source: "Make me explain the idea back before you continue."
entries.value: "Sa me bayyana the ra’ayi back kafin kai ci gaba."

entries.source: "Quiz me on this topic and wait for my answer."
entries.value: "Tambayoyi me a kan this batu da wait don my amsa."

entries.source: "What misconception would lead someone to my wrong answer?"
entries.value: "Abin da rashin fahimta would lead someone don my wrong amsa?"

entries.source: A simple test
entries.value: Wani mai sauƙi gwada

entries.source: A strong study loop looks like this:
entries.value: Wani mai ƙarfi madaukin karatu looks like this:

entries.source: AI can help you learn faster. It can also help you avoid learning entirely.
entries.value: AI zai iya taimakawa kai koyi sauri. It can also taimako kai guji learning entirely.

entries.source: AI tutor
entries.value: Malamin AI

entries.source: Ask for a hint. Do not ask for the final answer yet.
entries.value: Nemi a alama. kada a tambaya don the final amsa yet.

entries.source: Ask for a small hint.
entries.value: Nemi a small alama.

entries.source: Ask the AI to check that step.
entries.value: Tambayi the AI don bincika that mataki.

entries.source: At the end of a study session, ask yourself three questions:
entries.value: At the end na a karatu zama, tambaya yourself three tambayoyi:

entries.source: Attempt first. Spend three minutes trying without AI.
entries.value: Attempt farko. Spend three mintuna trying ba tare da AI.

entries.source: Avoid the copy trap
entries.value: Guji tarkon kwafe

entries.source: Build a loop
entries.value: Gina a madauki

entries.source: Can I do a new example?
entries.value: Shin I do a sabo example?

entries.source: Can I explain the idea without looking?
entries.value: Shin I bayyana the ra’ayi ba tare da looking?

entries.source: Different learning jobs need different flows. Use a homework coach for hints, a math step coach for problem solving, a Socratic tutor for deep understanding, flashcards for recall, and quizzes for pressure-tested practice.
entries.value: Different ayyukan koyo buƙata different gudanarwa. amfani da a aikin gida koci don alamomi, a lissafi mataki koci don problem solving, a Socratic malami don deep fahimta, katunan tunatarwa don tunawa, da tambayoyi don pressure-tested atisaye.

entries.source: Do I know what to practice next?
entries.value: Shin I know abin da don atisaye na gaba?

entries.source: Explain your move. Tell the AI why you chose the next step.
entries.value: Bayyana naka move. Tell the AI me ya sa kai chose the na gaba mataki.

entries.source: Fix the misconception.
entries.value: Fix the rashin fahimta.

entries.source: For writing, that means using Writing Coach to improve a thesis, structure, or paragraph while keeping your voice. For code, it means using Code Tutor to explain errors, ask you to predict output, and help you debug rather than pasting a complete solution. For exams, it means using Exam Prep Planner to decide what to review first and how to test yourself.
entries.value: Don rubutu, that means ta amfani da rubutu koci don inganta a thesis, structure, ko paragraph while keeping naka voice. don lamba, it means ta amfani da lamba malami don bayyana errors, tambaya kai don predict output, da taimako kai gyara kuskuren lamba rather fiye da pasting a complete solution. don exams, it means ta amfani da shirin jarabawa mai tsarawa don decide abin da don bita farko da yadda don gwada yourself.

entries.source: Good prompts for honest studying
entries.value: Good saƙonni don gaskiya studying

entries.source: Here is a slightly stronger version you can reuse:
entries.value: Here shi ne a slightly stronger version kai can reuse:

entries.source: How to study with AI without cheating yourself
entries.value: Yadda don karatu tare da AI ba tare da cheating yourself

entries.source: If the answer is yes, AI helped you study. If the answer is no, the next move is not more reading. It is active practice. Open Learn Anything, Socratic Instruction, or the AI learning prompt library and turn the topic into a task you have to attempt.
entries.value: If the amsa shi ne yes, AI helped kai karatu. If the amsa shi ne no, the na gaba move ba ƙarin karatu. Shi ne mai aiki atisaye. bude koyi Anything, Socratic Instruction, ko the koyon AI dakin karatun saƙonni da turn the batu zuwa a task kai have don attempt.

entries.source: If the work is homework, start in Homework Coach. If it is math, use Math Step Coach and ask it to check one step at a time. If you are trying to understand a concept deeply, use Socratic Instruction and answer the questions before asking for a summary.
entries.value: If the work shi ne aikin gida, fara a cikin aikin gida koci. If Shi ne lissafi, amfani da lissafi mataki koci da tambaya it don bincika one mataki at a lokaci. If kai suna trying don fahimci a ra’ayi deeply, amfani da Socratic Instruction da amsa the tambayoyi kafin asking don a taƙaitawa.

entries.source: If you paste a homework question and copy the final answer, you may finish the task, but you have not built the skill. If you ask for a hint, explain your thinking, compare your answer, and try a similar problem, AI becomes a study partner instead of a shortcut.
entries.value: If kai paste a aikin gida tambaya da kwafe the final amsa, kai na iya kammala the task, amma kai have not an gina the skill. If kai tambaya don a alama, bayyana naka thinking, kwatanta naka amsa, da gwada a similar problem, AI becomes a karatu partner maimakon a shortcut.

entries.source: Practice a new version.
entries.value: Atisaye a sabo version.

entries.source: Practice transfer. Ask for a similar problem with different numbers, wording, or context.
entries.value: Atisaye transfer. tambaya don a similar problem tare da different numbers, wording, ko mahaɗi.

entries.source: Recall later. Turn the weak spot into a card in Flashcard Builder or a short quiz in Quiz Me On Trivia.
entries.value: Tunawa daga baya. Turn the wurin rauni zuwa a card a cikin katin tunatarwa mai gina ko a gajere tambayoyi a cikin tambayoyi Me a kan Trivia.

entries.source: Request diagnosis. Ask what your answer reveals about your understanding.
entries.value: Request diagnosis. tambaya abin da naka amsa reveals game da naka fahimta.

entries.source: study skills
entries.value: dabarun karatu

entries.source: Studying with AI works best when the tool keeps you in the driver's seat.
entries.value: Studying tare da AI works mafi kyau lokacin da the kayan aiki keeps kai a cikin the driver's seat.

entries.source: That is a warning sign. Recognition is not recall. Reading a clear explanation is not the same as being able to solve a new problem, write a paragraph, or answer a question under pressure.
entries.value: Wannan shi ne a warning sign. karramawa ba tunawa. karatu a bayyananne explanation ba the same as being able don warware a sabo problem, write a paragraph, ko amsa a tambaya under pressure.

entries.source: That loop is slower than copying, but it is much faster than staying confused.
entries.value: Wannan madauki shi ne slower fiye da copying, amma Shi ne much sauri fiye da staying confused.

entries.source: The copy trap is subtle. It does not always feel like cheating. Sometimes it feels like being efficient. The AI explains something beautifully, you nod along, and then you move on. Ten minutes later you cannot reproduce the idea without the answer in front of you.
entries.value: Tarkon kwafe shi ne subtle. It ba ya always feel like cheating. Sometimes it feels like being efficient. AI explains something beautifully, kai nod along, da sannan kai move a kan. Ten mintuna daga baya kai cannot reproduce the ra’ayi ba tare da the amsa a cikin front na kai.

entries.source: The difference is how you use it.
entries.value: Difference shi ne yadda kai amfani da it.

entries.source: The question is not whether AI should be part of studying. For many learners it already is. The better question is whether the tool is helping you build memory, judgment, and confidence, or quietly removing the exact struggle that makes learning stick.
entries.value: Tambaya ba whether AI ya kamata be part na studying. don many masu koyo it already shi ne. mafi kyau tambaya shi ne whether the kayan aiki shi ne helping kai gina ƙwaƙwalwa, judgment, da kwarin gwiwa, ko quietly removing the exact struggle that makes learning stick.

entries.source: The transfer step is the key. If you can only solve the exact problem the AI just walked through, you have learned the example. If you can solve a cousin of the problem, you are starting to learn the idea.
entries.value: Transfer mataki shi ne the muhimmi. If kai can only warware the exact problem the AI just walked ta hanyar, kai have learned the example. If kai can warware a cousin na the problem, kai suna farawa don koyi the ra’ayi.

entries.source: These are not magic words. They are guardrails. They keep the conversation focused on thinking instead of answer collection.
entries.value: Waɗannan ba su magic words. su suna guardrails. su keep the conversation mai mayar da hankali a kan thinking maimakon amsa collection.

entries.source: These prompts keep you active. They make the AI support your reasoning rather than replace it.
entries.value: Waɗannan saƙonni keep kai mai aiki. su sa the AI tallafi naka tunani rather fiye da replace it.

entries.source: Try prompts like these:
entries.value: Gwada saƙonni like these:

entries.source: Try the problem yourself.
entries.value: Gwada the problem yourself.

entries.source: Use AI as a coach for understanding, practice, and feedback instead of a shortcut that quietly removes the learning.
entries.value: Amfani da AI as a koci don fahimta, atisaye, da martani maimakon a shortcut that quietly removes the learning.

entries.source: Use AI for the thinking around the answer
entries.value: Amfani da AI don the thinking a kusa da the amsa

entries.source: Use prompts that make the AI slow down:
entries.value: Amfani da saƙonni that sa the AI slow down:

entries.source: Use the right mode
entries.value: Amfani da the daidai mode

entries.source: Use this rule: after any AI explanation, close the loop with output from your own mind. Write the idea back. Solve a fresh version. Make a flashcard. Teach it to an imaginary younger student. Ask for a quiz. The more the session asks you to produce, the less likely you are to fool yourself.
entries.value: Amfani da this rule: bayan any AI explanation, close the madauki tare da output daga naka own mind. Write the ra’ayi back. warware a fresh version. sa a katin tunatarwa. koyar it don an imaginary younger dalibi. tambaya don a tambayoyi. ƙarin the zama asks kai don produce, the less likely kai suna don fool yourself.

entries.source: Write your next step.
entries.value: Write naka na gaba mataki.
